```{r, echo=FALSE, results="hide"}
set.seed(sample.int(.Machine$integer.max, 1))

## Parametros
xbar1   <- round(runif(1, 3, 8), 2)
xbar2   <- round(runif(1, 1, 5), 2)
sigma1  <- sample(c(1, 2, 3), 1)
sigma2_v <- sample(c(1, 2, 3), 1)
n1      <- sample(c(36, 49, 64), 1)
n2      <- sample(c(36, 49, 64), 1)

## Respostas: erro padrao de Xbar1 - Xbar2
correct <- round(sqrt(sigma1^2 / n1 + sigma2_v^2 / n2), 4)
w1 <- round(abs(sqrt(sigma1^2 / n1) - sqrt(sigma2_v^2 / n2)), 4)
w2 <- round((sigma1 + sigma2_v) / sqrt(n1 + n2), 4)
w3 <- round(sqrt((sigma1^2 + sigma2_v^2) / (n1 + n2)), 4)
w4 <- round(sqrt(sigma1^2 * n1 + sigma2_v^2 * n2), 4)

all_vals <- c(correct, w1, w2, w3, w4)
# Garante cinco alternativas numericamente distintas após a parametrização.
integer_choices <- all(abs(all_vals - round(all_vals)) < sqrt(.Machine$double.eps))
choice_step <- if (integer_choices) 1 else max(1e-4, max(abs(all_vals), na.rm = TRUE) * 0.01)
while (anyDuplicated(all_vals)) {
  duplicate_position <- which(duplicated(all_vals))[1]
  all_vals[duplicate_position] <- max(all_vals, na.rm = TRUE) + choice_step
}
choices <- sample(all_vals)
sol_pos <- which(choices == correct)
sol_str <- paste(as.integer(seq_along(choices) == sol_pos), collapse = "")
```

Question
========
Duas populacoes independentes tem desvios padrao conhecidos. Dados: $\bar{X}_1 = `r xbar1`$, $\sigma_1 = `r sigma1`$, $n_1 = `r n1`$; $\bar{X}_2 = `r xbar2`$, $\sigma_2 = `r sigma2_v`$, $n_2 = `r n2`$. O erro padrao de $\bar{X}_1 - \bar{X}_2$ e:

Answerlist
----------
* `r choices[1]`
* `r choices[2]`
* `r choices[3]`
* `r choices[4]`
* `r choices[5]`

Solution
========
Para amostras independentes, $\text{Var}(\bar{X}_1 - \bar{X}_2) = \sigma_1^2/n_1 + \sigma_2^2/n_2$, portanto o erro padrao e:
$$\sqrt{\frac{`r sigma1`^2}{`r n1`} + \frac{`r sigma2_v`^2}{`r n2`}} = `r correct`.$$

Erros comuns: subtrair as variancias; somar os desvios antes de dividir; dividir pela soma dos tamanhos.

Meta-information
================
exname: Cap5S1Q7_IC_dif_medias
extype: schoice
exsolution: `r sol_str`
exshuffle: FALSE
